posts.author: dpk1994
posts.community: Ola Electric - Full Stack EV play?
posts.text: Just sharing an observation. Since the March 2026 when deliveries bounced back, there is a continuous accumulation going on. Last month it was particularly high, mutual funds holding in Ola stands at 8.5% as of June 2026. Notable is to also see a consistent love of Motilal Oswal fund house’s love for Ola . Looks like either mutual funds are taking contrarian bet or they know something that broader market may not know or can’t see. Discl: Invested, not a recommendation to buy or sell. posts.text: In their 3rd Gen products they wanted to transition battery from ‘Banana shaped battery that slide under the seat’ to rectangular shaped under the footstep. Refer to the pictures. I wanted to know whether this has been done. Thanks! posts.author: ValueV
posts.community: Ola Electric - Full Stack EV play?
posts.text: I am not sure if I got your question but there are 2 types of motor: 1. Hub motor (placed in center of back tire itself) 2. Mid-drive motor where motor is placed in center of vehicle and transmission happens through chain or belt to back tire from motor. Ola initially used hub motor than moved to mid-drive. (of course after lot of complaints) And in both case battery connects with motor but not within battery pack. Both are different components. Thanks!

posts.author: dpk1994
posts.community: Ola Electric - Full Stack EV play?
posts.text: I have a question regarding Ola gen 3 scooters. It does have mid mount motor and chain drive but during Sankalp event it was also said that motor and battery would be connected. This is different than their regular Banana shape battery pack. Could someone confirm if that is the case or they still have banana shaped battery? Screenshot 2026-07-06 at 10.05.03 PM 1920×1377 297 KB Also, I tried to find videos about issues in Ola Gen 3 scooters and roadster bikes; actually I couldn’t find anything other than very minor complaints. Most of the complaints are in Gen 1 and Gen 2. Have someone observed…

posts.author: dpk1994
posts.community: Ola Electric - Full Stack EV play?
posts.text: After selling my entire holding in November 2025 at around ₹41, at a small loss, I slowly started accumulating again from the ₹23-24 range in March 2026. Since the lows of March 2026, I can actually see a decent turnaround - maybe not a spectacular one yet, but certainly a meaningful improvement. Sales are currently trailing around 15,000 units per month. Looking at the momentum, crossing 17,000-18,000 units per month does not look unrealistic with some more effort. If Ola can sustain volumes above 15,000 units, the automotive segment should ideally start generating some cash. However, I perso…
